concept: Inception Score uses a pretrained classifier to evaluate generated images: good fakes should yield high-confidence predictions for a single class (clarity) while the whole set should span many classes (diversity).

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of an image classifier network?

options.text: It tries to label/categorize what the image shows
It randomly deletes pixels to test image strength
It only checks if the image is exactly black or white

exercises.explain: A classifier network helps us grade generator models by trying to recognize what is inside the generated fake images!

cards.title: The Ultimate Judge
cards.body: Imagine you draw a picture of a creature, and your friend immediately says, 'That is definitely a cat!' That quick, certain guess means your drawing is clear. In AI, we use a smart image classifier to judge our generated images in the exact same way!

The Inception Score (IS) is a metric that evaluates generated images using a pre-trained image classifier. It measures two main things: Clarity (each image should clearly look like one specific object) and Diversity (the generator should produce many different kinds of objects).
cards.title: What is Inception Score?
The Classroom Test
cards.body: Think of an art class. If a student draws a picture, and the teacher instantly knows it's a dog, that's high clarity. If the student draws 100 pictures, and they aren't all dogs—some are trees, cars, and apples—that's high diversity. A good artist does both!

Let's say a generator outputs 10 images. If the classifier looks at image #1 and is 99% sure it is a 'banana', that's great clarity! If the next 9 images are clear pictures of zebras, planes, and hats, the set has high diversity, resulting in a high Inception Score.
cards.title: Score Calculations
Perfect Score, Perfect Image?
cards.body: You might think a high score means the images look 100% real and perfect. Actually, the classifier can be fooled! An image might look like weird, colorful static to humans, but if the classifier is highly confident it is a 'monarch butterfly', the score will still be high.

cards.title: Score Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare what leads to a high Inception Score versus a low Inception Score when evaluating a batch of generated images.

left.text: Clear individual objects (high confidence) spanning many different categories.

right.text: Blurry, unrecognizable shapes (low confidence) or generating only one single type of object.

exercises.explain: The Inception Score wants individual images to have clear, single-class labels (clarity) and the whole group of images to cover many different labels (diversity).

options.text: High confidence for individual classes, and a wide variety of classes overall
Low confidence for all classes, and only generating one type of object
Images that contain every single object combined together into one shape

exercises.prompt: What two main qualities lead to a high Inception Score?
True or False: If a generator only produces blurry, unrecognizable blobs, its Inception Score will be low.
exercises.explain: True! If every single image generated looks like a blurry smudge, the classifier will be confused, resulting in a very low Inception Score.
Diversity means the generator does not get stuck making the exact same object over and over again.
exercises.sentence: A generator that only outputs identical looking cats lacks [blank] because it never creates other classes of images.
exercises.prompt: A generator that only outputs identical looking cats lacks ______ because it never creates other classes of images.
Which of these AI outputs would achieve a higher Inception Score?
exercises.explain: The set with various clear objects (pizza, dogs, cars) shows both clarity and diversity, which is exactly what a high Inception Score measures.

options.text: A batch of clear, distinct images representing 50 different animals and vehicles
A batch of 1,000 images that are all identical copies of a fuzzy grey circle
To make the generated images look more colorful
To classify the generated images and evaluate their clarity and variety
To delete images that do not contain humans

exercises.explain: A pre-trained classifier is used to evaluate the generated images by checking how confident it is about their content and how diverse the overall set is. Next, we will explore another popular metric: Frechet Inception Distance!
exercises.prompt: Why does the Inception Score method require a pre-trained classifier?

title: Inception Score: Using a Classifier
